title: Mixed Content Detected
description: The HTTPS page loads resources over insecure HTTP connections.
evidence: Found HTTP resource references on HTTPS page.
riskImpact: Resources loaded over HTTP on an HTTPS page can be intercepted by attackers.
explanation: Mixed content occurs when an HTTPS page includes resources fetched over HTTP. Browsers may block some types and warn about others.
fixSteps: Update all resource URLs to use HTTPS or protocol-relative URLs.
Use the CSP directive upgrade-insecure-requests.
Audit all external resource references.

title: Potential Open Redirect Parameters
description: The page contains URL parameters or JavaScript patterns commonly associated with open redirect vulnerabilities.
evidence: Found redirect-related patterns in page source.
riskImpact: Open redirects can be used in phishing attacks by making malicious URLs appear to originate from your trusted domain.
explanation: Open redirect vulnerabilities occur when a web application takes a user-supplied URL parameter and redirects to it without validation.
fixSteps: Validate all redirect URLs against an allowlist.
Use relative URLs for redirects.
Reject any redirect URL that points to an external domain.
Implement server-side URL validation.

codeExamples.label: Validation

title: Missing Subresource Integrity (SRI)
description: External scripts are loaded without Subresource Integrity hashes.
evidence: Found external scripts without integrity attribute.
riskImpact: If a CDN or third-party host is compromised, attackers could serve malicious scripts.
explanation: SRI allows browsers to verify that files fetched from CDNs haven't been tampered with.
fixSteps: Generate SRI hashes for all external scripts and stylesheets.
Add 'integrity' and 'crossorigin' attributes.
Use tools like srihash.org to generate hashes.

title: Form Submitting Over HTTP
description: One or more forms submit data over unencrypted HTTP.
riskImpact: Form data including passwords and personal information are transmitted in plaintext.
explanation: When a form's action URL uses HTTP instead of HTTPS, all submitted data is sent in plaintext.
fixSteps: Update all form action URLs to use HTTPS.
Use relative URLs for form actions.
Add upgrade-insecure-requests CSP directive.

title: Email Address Exposure
description: Email addresses were found in the page source.
evidence: Found email addresses in page source.
riskImpact: Exposed email addresses can be harvested by bots for spam or targeted phishing.
explanation: Bots constantly crawl websites looking for email addresses in the HTML source.
fixSteps: Replace plaintext emails with contact forms.
Use JavaScript to dynamically render email addresses.
Encode email addresses using HTML entities.
Consider Cloudflare email obfuscation.

codeExamples.label: React Obfuscation

title: Directory Listing Appears Enabled
description: The response contains patterns indicating directory listing is enabled.
riskImpact: Attackers can browse your server's file structure, discovering sensitive files.
explanation: Directory listing allows anyone to see all files in a directory when no index file is present.
fixSteps: Disable directory listing in your web server configuration.
Ensure every directory has an index file.
Review exposed directories for sensitive files.

title: Sensitive File References Detected
description: The page references files commonly associated with sensitive configuration or development artifacts.
riskImpact: References to sensitive files may indicate they are accessible.
explanation: Sensitive files like .env, .git/, and configuration files should never be referenced in public pages.
fixSteps: Remove all references to sensitive files from public HTML.
Block access in your web server configuration.
Ensure .env, .git, and other config files are in .gitignore.

codeExamples.label: Nginx
codeExamples.code:
location ~ /\. { deny all; return 404; }
location ~ \.(env|sql|bak|config|log)$ { deny all; return 404; }

title: Potentially Outdated JavaScript Libraries
description: The page references JavaScript libraries with known security vulnerabilities.
evidence: Detected outdated libraries with known CVEs.
riskImpact: Outdated libraries contain publicly known vulnerabilities that attackers can exploit.
explanation: Using outdated client-side libraries exposes your application to known attack vectors.
fixSteps: Update all JavaScript libraries to their latest stable versions.
Use npm audit or Snyk to track vulnerabilities.
Set up Dependabot or Renovate for automated updates.
Remove unused libraries.

codeExamples.label: npm audit
codeExamples.code:
npm audit
npm audit fix
npm audit fix --force

title: Sensitive Paths Exposed in Robots.txt
description: The robots.txt file reveals potentially sensitive directory paths.
evidence: Sensitive disallowed paths found in robots.txt.
riskImpact: Robots.txt effectively creates a map of sensitive areas for attackers to probe.
explanation: Robots.txt files are publicly accessible and listing sensitive paths tells attackers exactly where to look.
fixSteps: Remove sensitive paths from robots.txt.
Ensure all sensitive endpoints require proper authentication.
Use auth and authorization rather than path hiding.

title: CMS / Technology Fingerprinting
description: The site exposes CMS or technology stack details that aid attacker reconnaissance.
riskImpact: Knowing the CMS type and version allows attackers to search for specific exploits.
explanation: CMS fingerprinting reveals the underlying technology, version, and sometimes plugin information.
fixSteps: Remove or obscure the generator meta tag.
Hide version numbers from public-facing pages.
Keep your CMS and all plugins updated.
Remove default installation files.

title: Missing security.txt File
description: No reference to a security.txt file was found.
riskImpact: Security researchers may not know how to responsibly report vulnerabilities.
explanation: The security.txt file (RFC 9116) provides a standardized way for security researchers to find contact information.
fixSteps: Create a /.well-known/security.txt file.
Include Contact, Preferred-Languages, Expires fields.
Sign the file with PGP if possible.

title: Potentially Dangerous Inline JavaScript
description: The page contains inline JavaScript with potentially dangerous patterns like eval(), document.write().
evidence: Found inline script blocks with dangerous patterns.
riskImpact: Dangerous JavaScript patterns can be exploited via XSS.
explanation: Patterns like eval(), document.write(), and direct innerHTML assignment with user input are common XSS vectors.
fixSteps: Replace eval() with JSON.parse().
Use textContent instead of innerHTML.
Move inline scripts to external files.
Use DOMPurify to sanitize HTML.

codeExamples.label: Safe alternatives

title: Reverse Tabnabbing Vulnerability
description: Links with target="_blank" are missing rel="noopener".
evidence: Anchor tags use target="_blank" without rel="noopener".
riskImpact: The opened page gains access to window.opener, allowing it to redirect your page to a phishing site.
explanation: When a link opens a new tab with target='_blank', the new page receives a reference to window.opener.
fixSteps: Add rel="noopener noreferrer" to all links with target="_blank".

title: JavaScript Source Maps Exposed
description: Source map references found in the page.
evidence: Detected sourceMappingURL directives or .js.map file references.
riskImpact: Source maps reveal original variable names, function logic, comments, and application structure.
explanation: Source maps are generated during the build process to aid debugging. In production, they expose your entire unminified source code.
fixSteps: Disable source map generation in production builds.
Upload source maps privately to your error tracking service.
Configure your bundler to strip sourceMappingURL comments in production.

title: Sensitive Information in HTML Comments
description: HTML comments containing potentially sensitive information detected.
riskImpact: HTML comments are visible to anyone viewing page source.
explanation: Developers often leave TODO notes, debug information, or temporary credentials in HTML comments.
fixSteps: Remove all HTML comments containing sensitive information.
Use a build process that strips comments.
Move debug notes to internal documentation.

title: Internal/Private IP Addresses Exposed
description: Internal or private IP addresses found in the page source.
riskImpact: Internal IP addresses reveal your network topology.
explanation: Private IP addresses and localhost references in public-facing pages leak information about your internal network structure.
fixSteps: Remove hardcoded internal IP addresses.
Use environment-specific configuration.
Review reverse proxy configurations.

title: Debug Mode or Error Information Exposed
description: Debug/error indicators that reveal internal application details detected.
evidence: Debug headers, stack traces, or error messages found.
riskImpact: Debug output exposes internal file paths, framework versions, database types, and application logic.
explanation: Debug mode and verbose error messages are invaluable during development but catastrophic in production.
fixSteps: Disable debug mode in production.
Configure custom error pages.
Remove fingerprinting headers.
Set up error monitoring (Sentry).

codeExamples.label: Express.js

title: Insecure Iframe Sources on HTTPS Page
description: Iframes loading content over HTTP on an HTTPS page.
evidence: Found iframes using http:// src on HTTPS page.
riskImpact: HTTP iframes on HTTPS pages create mixed content, allowing MITM attackers to modify iframe content.
explanation: Browsers display your page as secure but an HTTP iframe can be intercepted and modified by network attackers.
fixSteps: Change all iframe src attributes to use HTTPS.
Use CSP: frame-src https:.
Consider using the sandbox attribute.

title: Authentication Tokens Exposed in Page Source
description: Tokens or session identifiers exposed in the HTML source.
evidence: JWT tokens, Bearer tokens, or session IDs found in page source.
riskImpact: Exposed tokens allow session hijacking.
explanation: Authentication tokens should never appear in HTML source. They can be captured by browser extensions, cached by CDNs, or logged by proxies.
fixSteps: Store tokens in HTTP-only Secure cookies.
If tokens must be passed to JavaScript, inject them via a secure API call.
Never pass tokens in URL query parameters.

codeExamples.label: Secure cookie
codeExamples.code: response.cookies.set('session', token, { httpOnly: true, secure: true, sameSite: 'lax', maxAge: 86400, path: '/' });

title: Missing Autocomplete Attributes on Sensitive Fields
description: Sensitive input fields without proper autocomplete attributes.
evidence: Password or credit card fields missing autocomplete directives.
riskImpact: Browsers may cache sensitive form data on shared or compromised devices.
explanation: The autocomplete attribute tells browsers how to handle form autofill.
fixSteps: Add autocomplete="new-password" for registration fields.
Add autocomplete="current-password" for login fields.
Add appropriate autocomplete values for payment fields.

title: Forms Targeting New Windows
description: Forms with target="_blank" can be abused for phishing via reverse tabnabbing.
evidence: Form element(s) submit to a new window/tab.
riskImpact: Form submissions opening new windows can confuse users and be exploited.
explanation: Forms that open results in new tabs create the same reverse tabnabbing risk as anchor links.
fixSteps: Remove target="_blank" from forms unless necessary.
Consider handling form submission via fetch instead.

codeExamples.label: AJAX submission

title: Meta Refresh Redirect Detected
description: Meta refresh redirects can be abused for open redirect attacks and phishing.
evidence: Meta http-equiv="refresh" tag(s) with URL redirects.
riskImpact: Meta refresh redirects bypass standard navigation controls.
explanation: Meta refresh tags automatically redirect users after a delay. Unlike server-side redirects, they execute in the browser.
fixSteps: Replace meta refresh with server-side 301/302 redirects.
Never allow user-controlled input in meta refresh URLs.

title: Insecure Base Tag Detected
description: The page uses a <base> tag with an HTTP URL, making all relative URLs resolve insecurely.
evidence: Found <base> tag(s) pointing to insecure HTTP origin.
riskImpact: All relative links, scripts, images resolve against the insecure base URL.
explanation: The HTML <base> tag sets the base URL for all relative URLs in the document.
fixSteps: Change the <base> tag to use HTTPS.
Remove the <base> tag if not needed.
Implement CSP base-uri directive.

title: Overly Permissive Permissions-Policy
description: The Permissions-Policy header grants wildcard (*) access to browser features.
riskImpact: Wildcard permissions allow any embedded content to access sensitive browser features.
explanation: Using * as the allowlist defeats the purpose entirely.
fixSteps: Replace * with 'self'.
Explicitly list only the origins that need access.
Disable features you don't use with empty allowlists.

title: postMessage Listener Without Origin Validation
description: postMessage event listeners don't appear to validate the message origin.
riskImpact: Any website can send messages to your page via postMessage.
explanation: If the receiving page doesn't verify event.origin, any malicious page can send crafted messages.
fixSteps: Always check event.origin against a whitelist.
Validate the structure and type of event.data.
Use event.source to verify the sender.

title: Sensitive Endpoints Referenced in Page Source
description: References to potentially sensitive endpoints in the page source.
riskImpact: References to admin panels, debug endpoints help attackers map your attack surface.
explanation: Even if endpoints are protected, exposing their paths gives attackers specific targets.
fixSteps: Remove references to internal/admin endpoints from client-side code.
Use server-side rendering for admin navigation.
Move API documentation behind authentication.

title: Dangerous HTML Attributes Detected
description: Potentially dangerous HTML attributes like inline event handlers, javascript: URIs.
riskImpact: Inline event handlers and javascript: URIs are common XSS vectors.
explanation: Inline event handlers, javascript: protocol links, and data: URIs embedding HTML/JS are all classic XSS injection points.
fixSteps: Remove all javascript: protocol links.
Move inline event handlers to external JavaScript files.
Implement CSP with 'unsafe-inline' disabled.

codeExamples.label: Safe pattern
codeExamples.code: Click

title: JWT Token Exposed in URL
description: A JSON Web Token was found embedded in a URL within the page HTML.
riskImpact: The token appears in browser history, proxy logs, server logs, and Referer headers.
explanation: JWTs in URLs persist in numerous locations, creating many avenues for token theft.
fixSteps: Transmit tokens in HTTP headers (Authorization: Bearer) instead of URLs.
Use HTTP-only secure cookies.
Implement short-lived single-use tokens.

codeExamples.label: Fetch with header

title: Sensitive Information in Meta Tags
description: Meta tags that expose sensitive or unnecessary information.
riskImpact: Meta tag contents are visible in page source.
explanation: Generator tags reveal exact software versions with known CVEs. CSRF tokens in meta tags are accessible to any script.
fixSteps: Remove generator meta tags in production.
Move CSRF tokens to HTTP-only cookies.
Never store API keys in meta tags.

title: Sensitive Data Stored in Browser Storage APIs
description: JavaScript stores sensitive values (tokens, secrets) in localStorage or sessionStorage.
evidence: Sensitive data in browser storage APIs.
riskImpact: Any XSS vulnerability allows attackers to steal all data from browser storage.
explanation: Web Storage APIs are fully accessible to JavaScript. If an attacker exploits any XSS, they can read all stored values.
fixSteps: Move authentication tokens to HTTP-only, Secure, SameSite cookies.
Never store passwords, API keys, or PII in browser storage.
Implement CSP to reduce XSS risk.

codeExamples.label: Secure cookie instead
codeExamples.code:
// BAD: localStorage.setItem('token', jwt);
// GOOD: Set via server Set-Cookie: session=abc; HttpOnly; Secure; SameSite=Strict

title: CDN Resources Loaded Without Subresource Integrity
description: Scripts loaded from CDNs lack integrity attributes.
riskImpact: A compromised CDN could serve malicious scripts to all your users.
explanation: SRI lets you provide a cryptographic hash of the expected file content. The browser verifies before executing.
fixSteps: Add integrity and crossorigin attributes to all CDN resources.
Consider self-hosting critical libraries as an alternative.

title: Suspicious Content in Open Graph Tags
description: Open Graph meta tags contain suspicious content that could indicate injection.
riskImpact: Malicious OG content can be exploited by link preview services.
explanation: Some preview renderers process OG values in ways that can be exploited.
fixSteps: Sanitize all Open Graph tag content.
Validate og:url and og:image point to your own domains.
Never include user-controlled input in OG tags without encoding.

title: Service Worker Registered Over Insecure HTTP
description: A service worker is being registered from an HTTP URL.
riskImpact: A MITM attacker could replace the service worker with malicious code that persists.
explanation: Service workers intercept all network requests. A compromised one gives attackers persistent control.
fixSteps: Serve the service worker file over HTTPS.
Use relative URLs for registration.

title: Window.opener Access Detected
description: JavaScript accesses window.opener properties, exploitable for reverse tabnabbing.
riskImpact: Can redirect the parent tab to a phishing page without the user noticing.
explanation: When a page is opened via window.open() or target='_blank', it may have a reference to the opening window.
fixSteps: Add rel='noopener noreferrer' to external links.
Set window.opener = null at the top of pages.
Use Cross-Origin-Opener-Policy: same-origin.

title: Insecure WebSocket Connection (ws://)
description: WebSocket connections using unencrypted ws:// instead of wss://.
riskImpact: Unencrypted WebSocket connections can be intercepted. WebSocket connections don't enforce same-origin policy.
explanation: ws:// transmits data in plaintext. WebSocket connections aren't bound by same-origin policy, making them vulnerable to cross-site hijacking.
fixSteps: Use wss:// for all WebSocket connections.
Validate the Origin header on the server.
Implement CSRF token verification in the handshake.

codeExamples.label: Secure WebSocket
codeExamples.code:
// BAD: new WebSocket('ws://example.com/feed');
// GOOD: new WebSocket('wss://example.com/feed');

title: Deprecated document.domain Usage Detected
description: The page sets document.domain, a deprecated practice that relaxes same-origin policy.
riskImpact: Setting document.domain weakens same-origin isolation.
explanation: document.domain was historically used for cross-subdomain communication. Chrome has deprecated it. Use postMessage instead.
fixSteps: Replace document.domain with postMessage().
Use CORS headers for cross-origin API requests.
Consider Channel Messaging API.

codeExamples.label: Use postMessage

title: Weak or Broken Cryptography Detected
description: Weak or broken cryptographic algorithms in client-side JavaScript.
riskImpact: Weak cryptography provides a false sense of security. MD5/SHA-1 can be forged, DES/RC4 broken, Math.random() is predictable.
explanation: Cryptographic algorithms become weak as computing power increases. MD5 collisions can be generated in seconds.
fixSteps: Replace MD5/SHA-1 with SHA-256 or SHA-3.
Replace DES/RC4 with AES-256-GCM.
Use crypto.getRandomValues() instead of Math.random().
Use the SubtleCrypto API.

codeExamples.label: Web Crypto API

title: Password Fields Block Pasting
description: Password fields have paste functionality disabled, discouraging password manager use.
riskImpact: Users cannot paste from password managers, leading to weaker passwords.
explanation: Both NCSC (UK) and NIST guidelines explicitly recommend against disabling paste on password fields.
fixSteps: Remove all onpaste event handlers from password fields.
Allow paste on all form fields, especially password fields.

codeExamples.label: Allow paste
codeExamples.code:
<!-- BAD -->
<input type="password" onpaste="return false">
<!-- GOOD -->
<input type="password" autocomplete="current-password">

title: Application Error Messages Exposed to Users
description: Detailed error messages visible in the page HTML.
riskImpact: Detailed errors reveal file paths, database types, library versions.
explanation: Verbose error messages reveal the technology stack and internal structure.
fixSteps: Configure production to display generic error pages.
Log detailed errors server-side only.
Implement custom error handlers.

codeExamples.label: Next.js error handling

title: Server-Side XML Parsing Detected
description: Server-side XML parsing library usage found. Ensure external entity processing is disabled.
riskImpact: If external entities are not disabled, XXE attacks can read local files or perform SSRF.
explanation: Server-side XML parsers can be vulnerable to XXE if not properly configured.
fixSteps: Disable external entity processing.
Validate that noent: false and nonet: true are set.
Consider using JSON instead of XML.

codeExamples.label: Safe xml2js config
codeExamples.code: const parser = new xml2js.Parser({ strict: true, normalize: true, xmlns: false });

title: Potential Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) Vectors
description: User-controlled input may be used in server-side HTTP requests.
riskImpact: SSRF allows attackers to access internal resources, cloud metadata services, or internal networks.
explanation: SSRF occurs when an application fetches a remote resource without validating the user-supplied URL.
fixSteps: Validate URLs against an allowlist.
Block requests to private IP ranges.
Disable redirects or validate redirect destinations.

codeExamples.label: SSRF prevention

title: GraphQL Introspection Enabled in Production
description: GraphQL introspection is enabled, exposing the entire API schema.
evidence: Found GraphQL introspection queries or GraphiQL interface.
riskImpact: Attackers can discover all queries, mutations, types, and fields.
explanation: Introspection is useful for development but should be disabled in production.
fixSteps: Disable introspection in production.
Use environment variables to conditionally enable.
Implement authentication before allowing introspection.
Consider persisted queries.

codeExamples.label: Disable introspection
codeExamples.code: const server = new ApolloServer({ typeDefs, resolvers, introspection: process.env.NODE_ENV !== 'production' });

title: External Stylesheets Missing SRI
description: External stylesheets are loaded without Subresource Integrity hashes.
riskImpact: A compromised CDN could serve malicious CSS that alters page appearance for phishing or exfiltrates data via CSS selectors.
explanation: While less dangerous than script injection, malicious CSS can overlay fake login forms, hide content, or exfiltrate data via attribute selectors.
fixSteps: Add integrity attributes to all external stylesheet <link> tags.
Use crossorigin='anonymous' alongside.

title: Iframes Missing Sandbox Attribute
description: Multiple iframes found without the sandbox attribute, allowing embedded content full capabilities.
riskImpact: Unsandboxed iframes have full access to browser features, forms, scripts, and popups.
explanation: The sandbox attribute restricts what an iframe can do. Without it, embedded content has nearly the same capabilities as the parent page.
fixSteps: Add the sandbox attribute to all iframes.
Only enable needed capabilities: allow-scripts, allow-same-origin, etc.

title: Password Fields Missing Name/Autocomplete Attributes
description: Password input fields are missing name or autocomplete attributes, hindering password manager functionality.
evidence: Password field(s) missing name or autocomplete attributes.
riskImpact: Password managers may not correctly identify or fill these fields, discouraging their use.
explanation: Password managers rely on input name and autocomplete attributes to correctly identify and autofill fields.
fixSteps: Add name and autocomplete attributes to all password fields.
Use autocomplete='current-password' for login and 'new-password' for registration.

title: POST Forms Without CSRF Tokens
description: POST form(s) found without apparent CSRF token fields. Note: This check skips framework apps (Next.js, Nuxt) that handle CSRF differently.
evidence: POST form(s) without CSRF token fields.
riskImpact: Without CSRF tokens, attackers can trick users into submitting forms from malicious sites.
explanation: CSRF attacks forge requests from authenticated users. CSRF tokens ensure form submissions originate from your site.
fixSteps: Add a hidden CSRF token field to all POST forms.
Validate the token on the server for every form submission.
Use the SameSite cookie attribute as additional protection.

title: API Version Information Exposed
description: API version numbers or build identifiers are exposed in headers or page source.
riskImpact: Version numbers help attackers identify specific vulnerabilities for that version.
explanation: Exposing exact version numbers aids attacker reconnaissance. Remove or obfuscate version info in production.
fixSteps: Remove version headers like X-API-Version.
Don't include build IDs in client-facing code.
Use generic version references if needed.

codeExamples.label: Remove headers

title: Missing HTML lang Attribute
description: The <html> tag does not include a lang attribute, impacting accessibility and SEO.
evidence: The <html> tag does not include a lang attribute.
riskImpact: Screen readers may not announce content in the correct language. Search engines may misidentify the page language.
explanation: The lang attribute on <html> tells browsers and assistive technologies the primary language of the page.
fixSteps: Add lang attribute to the <html> tag: <html lang="en">.
Use appropriate BCP 47 language codes.

title: Forms Submitting to External Domains
description: Form(s) found that submit data to external third-party domains (excluding known payment providers).
riskImpact: Form data may be sent to untrusted external servers.
explanation: Forms with external action URLs send user data outside your domain. While legitimate for payment forms, unexpected external submissions may indicate compromise.
fixSteps: Review all external form actions for legitimacy.
Use server-side proxying for third-party form submissions.
Add CSP form-action to restrict allowed targets.

title: Sensitive Data in Browser Storage
description: Sensitive data (tokens, passwords, API keys) being stored in localStorage or sessionStorage.
evidence: Sensitive data stored in browser storage.
riskImpact: Any XSS vulnerability allows complete theft of stored sensitive data.
explanation: Browser storage APIs have no access controls. Any script on the page can read all stored values.
fixSteps: Move tokens to HTTP-only cookies.
Never store passwords or API keys client-side.

codeExamples.label: Use cookies
codeExamples.code:
// Instead of localStorage, use HTTP-only cookies
response.cookies.set('session', token, { httpOnly: true, secure: true, sameSite: 'strict' });

title: Viewport Prevents User Zoom
description: The viewport meta tag disables user scaling (user-scalable=no or maximum-scale=1), which is an accessibility issue.
riskImpact: Users with visual impairments cannot zoom the page, violating WCAG accessibility guidelines.
explanation: Disabling zoom creates an accessibility barrier for users who need to enlarge content.
fixSteps: Remove user-scalable=no from viewport meta.
Remove or increase maximum-scale.
Allow natural pinch-to-zoom behavior.

title: Stack Trace Exposed in Page Source
description: A stack trace with file paths and line numbers was found in the page source.
evidence: Stack trace with file paths and line numbers in page source.
riskImpact: Stack traces reveal exact file paths, function names, and line numbers that attackers use to understand application internals.
explanation: Stack traces should never be visible in production. They reveal the complete execution path and internal file structure.
fixSteps: Configure production error handling to suppress stack traces.
Use error monitoring services (Sentry) for server-side tracking.
Return generic error messages to clients.

codeExamples.label: Safe error response

title: Hardcoded IP Addresses in Page Source
description: Multiple distinct IP addresses found hardcoded in page source (excluding localhost and common non-sensitive IPs).
evidence: IP addresses found in page source.
riskImpact: Hardcoded IPs reveal server infrastructure and network topology.
explanation: IP addresses in client-facing code suggest hardcoded infrastructure references that should use DNS or environment variables.
fixSteps: Replace hardcoded IPs with domain names.
Use environment variables for server addresses.
Configure DNS properly.

codeExamples.label: Use DNS
codeExamples.code: const API = process.env.API_URL; // Not a hardcoded IP

title: document.write() Usage Detected
description: document.write()/document.writeln() usage found. These are deprecated and can be exploited for XSS.
riskImpact: document.write() can be hijacked to inject malicious content if any input is attacker-controlled.
explanation: document.write() is deprecated, blocks HTML parsing, and is a common XSS vector.
fixSteps: Replace document.write() with DOM APIs (createElement, appendChild).
Use innerHTML with DOMPurify if HTML insertion is needed.

codeExamples.label: Safe alternative

title: Excessive Third-Party Domain Connections
description: The page connects to a large number of distinct third-party domains (10+), increasing attack surface.
riskImpact: Each third-party domain is a potential attack vector. If any is compromised, it can affect your users.
explanation: Every external domain you load resources from expands your attack surface. A compromise of any CDN, analytics, or advertising domain can lead to script injection.
fixSteps: Audit all third-party connections and remove unnecessary ones.
Self-host critical resources where possible.
Use SRI for all external scripts.
Implement a strict CSP to limit allowed domains.

title: Text Inputs Missing maxlength Attribute
description: Multiple text inputs and textareas found without maxlength, allowing unbounded input.
riskImpact: Unbounded input fields can be used for denial-of-service or buffer-based attacks on backend systems.
explanation: Without maxlength, users (or attackers) can submit extremely long strings that may cause performance issues or exploit backend vulnerabilities.
fixSteps: Add maxlength attribute to all text inputs and textareas.
Validate input length on the server side as well.

title: Images Missing Lazy Loading
description: Many images found without loading='lazy' attribute, impacting page load performance.
riskImpact: All images load immediately, increasing initial page load time and bandwidth usage.
explanation: Native lazy loading defers offscreen images until the user scrolls near them, improving initial load performance.
fixSteps: Add loading='lazy' to images that are below the fold.
Keep loading='eager' for above-the-fold images.

title: Links with target="_blank" Missing rel="noopener"
description: Multiple links with target="_blank" are missing rel="noopener", enabling reverse tabnabbing.
riskImpact: The opened page can redirect the original tab to a phishing page via window.opener.
explanation: Modern browsers mitigate this by default, but explicit rel="noopener" ensures safety across all browsers.
fixSteps: Add rel="noopener noreferrer" to all external links with target="_blank".

title: Sensitive Cookies Missing __Host-/__Secure- Prefix
description: Session or authentication cookies do not use the __Host- or __Secure- cookie prefix.
riskImpact: Without these prefixes, cookies may be vulnerable to overwriting by subdomains or non-HTTPS contexts, enabling session fixation attacks.
explanation: __Host- prefix ensures the cookie is set from a secure origin, has Path=/, and cannot be overwritten by subdomains. __Secure- ensures the cookie was set over HTTPS. These are defense-in-depth measures against cookie injection.
fixSteps: Rename sensitive cookies to use __Host- prefix (strictest) or __Secure- prefix.
Ensure the cookie has Secure, Path=/, and no Domain attribute for __Host-.

title: Deprecated Feature-Policy Header
description: Feature-Policy header is used without the modern Permissions-Policy replacement.
evidence: Feature-Policy header present but not Permissions-Policy.
riskImpact: Feature-Policy is deprecated and may not be respected by modern browsers. Your intended permissions restrictions may not be enforced.
explanation: Feature-Policy was renamed to Permissions-Policy with a different syntax. While some browsers still read Feature-Policy, it's being phased out.
fixSteps: Replace Feature-Policy with Permissions-Policy using the new syntax.
You can send both headers during the transition period.

title: Cookie SameSite=None Without Secure
description: Cookie has SameSite=None but lacks Secure flag.
riskImpact: Cookie will be rejected by modern browsers.
explanation: Browsers require SameSite=None cookies to also have the Secure attribute.
fixSteps: Add Secure flag to all SameSite=None cookies.

title: Cookie With Excessively Long Expiration
description: Cookie set to expire far in the future (over 1 year).
riskImpact: Long-lived cookies increase the window for session theft.
explanation: Cookies that persist for years are a security risk.
fixSteps: Set reasonable max-age values; use session cookies where possible.

title: AWS Metadata Endpoint Reference
description: Reference to AWS metadata endpoint (169.254.169.254) detected.
riskImpact: SSRF to metadata endpoint can steal IAM credentials.
explanation: The AWS metadata service provides instance credentials.
fixSteps: Block metadata access from applications; use IMDSv2.

title: .git Directory Paths Exposed
description: .git directory paths found in page source.
riskImpact: Exposed .git allows downloading entire source code history.
explanation: If .git is accessible, attackers can reconstruct your entire repository.
fixSteps: Block access to .git directory in web server config.

title: .env File Reference Detected
description: References to .env file found in page source.
riskImpact: Exposed .env files contain database credentials, API keys, and secrets.
explanation: .env files should never be web-accessible.
fixSteps: Block .env access in web server config.

title: Backup File References Detected
description: References to backup files found.
riskImpact: Backup files may contain source code or database dumps.
explanation: Backup files on servers can expose previous code versions.
fixSteps: Remove backup files from production servers.

title: phpinfo() Page Exposed
description: phpinfo() page or reference detected.
riskImpact: phpinfo() exposes complete server configuration, PHP settings, and environment variables.
explanation: This diagnostic page should never be accessible in production.
fixSteps: Remove phpinfo() files from production.
Block access in web server config.

title: WordPress Admin Paths Exposed
description: WordPress admin/login page paths exposed.
riskImpact: Reveals WordPress installation and admin panel location.
explanation: Default WordPress admin paths are common brute-force targets.
fixSteps: Use a security plugin to hide or rename wp-login.php.
Implement rate limiting on login attempts.

title: GraphQL Endpoint Exposed
description: GraphQL endpoint or introspection references found.
riskImpact: Reveals API structure and available queries.
explanation: GraphQL introspection lets anyone discover your full API schema.
fixSteps: Disable introspection in production.
Implement query depth limiting.

title: API Documentation Publicly Accessible
description: Swagger/OpenAPI documentation endpoints referenced.
riskImpact: API docs reveal all endpoints, parameters, and data models.
explanation: Public API documentation helps attackers understand your API surface.
fixSteps: Restrict API docs access to authenticated users.

title: SQL Error Messages Exposed
description: SQL error messages found in response.
riskImpact: SQL errors reveal database type, table names, and query structure.
explanation: Unhandled SQL errors expose database internals useful for SQL injection.
fixSteps: Catch database errors and show generic messages.
Use parameterized queries.

title: PHP Error Messages Exposed
description: PHP error or warning messages found.
evidence: PHP errors detected in page output.
riskImpact: PHP errors reveal file paths and code structure.
explanation: Visible PHP errors expose server internals.
fixSteps: Set display_errors=Off and log_errors=On.

title: ASP.NET Error Page Exposed
description: ASP.NET error page detected.
riskImpact: Error pages reveal framework version, file paths, and stack traces.
explanation: Default ASP.NET error pages show detailed diagnostic information.
fixSteps: Configure custom error pages in web.config.

title: Django Debug Page Exposed
description: Django debug page with framework details detected.
riskImpact: Django debug pages expose settings, database configuration, and full stack traces.
explanation: Django DEBUG mode should never be enabled in production.
fixSteps: Set DEBUG=False in settings.py.

title: Laravel Debug Page Exposed
description: Laravel Whoops debug page detected.
riskImpact: Exposes environment variables, database credentials, and full stack traces.
explanation: Laravel debug mode shows complete application internals.
fixSteps: Set APP_DEBUG=false in .env.

title: Spring Boot Actuator Endpoints Exposed
description: Spring Boot Actuator endpoints found in page source.
riskImpact: Actuator endpoints expose env vars, beans, mappings, and health status.
explanation: Spring Boot Actuator provides operational endpoints that should be secured.
fixSteps: Restrict actuator endpoints to internal access only.
Use Spring Security to protect actuator.

title: Form Submits to HTTP on HTTPS Page
description: HTTPS page contains form posting to HTTP endpoint.
evidence: Form action pointing to http:// URL.
riskImpact: Form data transmitted unencrypted.
explanation: Mixed content forms leak sensitive data over insecure connections.
fixSteps: Ensure all form actions use HTTPS.

title: Iframes Without Sandbox Attribute
description: Iframe elements missing sandbox attribute.
riskImpact: Unsandboxed iframes have full page permissions.
explanation: Sandbox restricts what embedded content can do.
fixSteps: Add sandbox attribute with minimal permissions.

title: External Scripts Missing SRI
description: External scripts loaded without integrity hash.
evidence: Script(s) with external src missing integrity.
riskImpact: Compromised CDN can inject malicious code.
explanation: SRI ensures scripts match expected content hashes.
fixSteps: Add integrity and crossorigin attributes to external scripts.

title: Excessive Inline Event Handlers
description: Multiple inline event handler attributes found.
riskImpact: Inline handlers bypass CSP and complicate security auditing.
explanation: Inline event handlers are equivalent to inline scripts for XSS purposes.
fixSteps: Use addEventListener() instead of inline handlers.

title: Deprecated document.domain Usage
description: document.domain assignment found.
riskImpact: Relaxes same-origin policy between subdomains.
explanation: document.domain is deprecated and weakens isolation.
fixSteps: Use postMessage() for cross-subdomain communication.

title: postMessage() With Wildcard Origin
description: postMessage called with * origin target.
riskImpact: Sensitive data sent to any listening window.
explanation: Using * as target origin means any page can receive the message.
fixSteps: Specify the exact target origin instead of *.

title: JWT Token in Page Source
description: JSON Web Token found in HTML source.
riskImpact: Exposed tokens allow session hijacking.
explanation: JWTs in page source can be stolen by XSS attacks.
fixSteps: Never embed JWTs in HTML; use HttpOnly cookies.

title: Private Key Material Exposed
description: Private key found in page source.
riskImpact: Private keys allow impersonation, decryption, and signing.
explanation: Private keys must never be exposed in client-facing code.
fixSteps: Immediately rotate the exposed key.
Store keys in secure key management systems.

title: Base64 Credentials in Page Source
description: Basic authentication credentials (Base64) found.
riskImpact: Base64 credentials are trivially decodable.
explanation: Basic auth headers encode credentials in Base64 which is not encryption.
fixSteps: Remove hardcoded credentials from page source.
Use token-based authentication.

title: Database Connection String Exposed
description: Database connection string with credentials found.
riskImpact: Direct database access with leaked credentials.
explanation: Connection strings contain hostname, username, password, and database name.
fixSteps: Move connection strings to environment variables.
Rotate exposed credentials immediately.

title: AWS S3 Bucket Reference Exposed
description: AWS S3 bucket reference found in page source.
riskImpact: S3 buckets may be publicly accessible or misconfigured.
explanation: Exposed bucket names can be tested for misconfigured permissions.
fixSteps: Use CloudFront or a CDN in front of S3 buckets.
Ensure proper bucket policies.

title: Firebase Configuration Exposed
description: Firebase configuration object found in page source.
evidence: Firebase config with API key detected.
riskImpact: While Firebase keys are designed to be public, exposed config may indicate unsecured backend rules.
explanation: Firebase config is semi-public but should be paired with security rules.
fixSteps: Ensure Firestore/RTDB security rules are properly configured.

title: Source Map References Detected
description: JavaScript source map URL references found.
riskImpact: Source maps reveal original unminified source code.
explanation: Source maps in production allow viewing original code with variable names and comments.
fixSteps: Remove sourceMappingURL in production builds.

title: Potential Open Redirect Parameters
description: URL redirect parameters found in page links.
riskImpact: Open redirects enable phishing attacks.
explanation: Unvalidated redirect parameters can send users to malicious sites.
fixSteps: Validate redirect URLs against an allowlist.
Use relative paths for redirects.

title: External Stylesheets Missing SRI
description: External stylesheets loaded without integrity hash.
riskImpact: Compromised CDN could serve malicious CSS.
explanation: While less dangerous than script injection, malicious CSS can overlay fake forms.
fixSteps: Add integrity and crossorigin to external stylesheet links.

title: Sensitive Fields Missing Autocomplete
description: Sensitive input fields without autocomplete attribute.
riskImpact: Browsers may cache or suggest sensitive data inappropriately.
explanation: autocomplete tells browsers how to handle field data.
fixSteps: Add appropriate autocomplete values to all sensitive fields.

title: Password Form Uses GET Method
description: Form with password field uses GET method.
riskImpact: Credentials appear in URL, browser history, and server logs.
explanation: GET requests encode form data in the URL query string.
fixSteps: Change form method to POST for all forms with sensitive data.

title: Meta Referrer Tag Leaks URLs
description: Meta referrer tag set to an unsafe value.
riskImpact: Full URLs including paths and query params leaked as referrer.
explanation: unsafe-url sends the complete URL to all destinations.
fixSteps: Use strict-origin-when-cross-origin or no-referrer.

title: Session Cookie Missing Security Flags
description: Session cookie missing HttpOnly, Secure, or SameSite.
riskImpact: Session can be stolen via XSS or sent over HTTP.
explanation: Security flags protect cookies from common attacks.
fixSteps: Add HttpOnly, Secure, and SameSite=Lax to session cookies.

title: Session ID in URL
description: Session identifier found in URL.
riskImpact: Session ID leaked in referrer, history, logs.
explanation: URL-based session IDs are vulnerable to session fixation.
fixSteps: Store session IDs only in cookies with proper flags.

title: Password in GET Request URL
description: Password parameter found in URL.
riskImpact: Credentials exposed in browser history, server logs, referrer.
explanation: GET parameters appear in URL and are logged everywhere.
fixSteps: Use POST for all forms with passwords.

title: Weak Password Length Constraint
description: Password field allows very short passwords.
riskImpact: Users can set easily guessable passwords.
explanation: Short passwords are vulnerable to brute force attacks.
fixSteps: Require minimum 8-12 characters for passwords.

title: Remember-Me Token in URL
description: Remember-me token exposed in URL.
riskImpact: Persistent auth token leaked in referrer and logs.
explanation: Remember-me tokens should be in cookies, not URLs.
fixSteps: Store remember-me tokens in secure, HttpOnly cookies.

title: OAuth URL Missing State Parameter
description: OAuth authorization URL without state parameter.
riskImpact: Vulnerable to CSRF attacks on OAuth flow.
explanation: State parameter prevents cross-site request forgery in OAuth.
fixSteps: Always include a cryptographically random state parameter.

title: Multiple API Versions Exposed
description: Multiple API version endpoints referenced.
riskImpact: Older API versions may have unpatched vulnerabilities.
explanation: Legacy API versions often lack security updates.
fixSteps: Sunset old API versions, redirect to latest.

title: Debug Endpoints Referenced
description: Debug or profiler endpoints found in source.
riskImpact: Debug endpoints often expose sensitive information.
explanation: Debug pages may show configs, stack traces, environment.
fixSteps: Remove or restrict debug endpoints in production.

title: Admin Endpoints Referenced
description: Admin or management endpoints found in source.
riskImpact: Reveals admin panel locations to attackers.
explanation: Knowing admin URLs helps attackers target them.
fixSteps: Use obscure paths, add authentication, IP restrictions.

title: Internal IP Address Exposed
description: Private/internal IP address in page source.
riskImpact: Reveals internal network topology.
explanation: Private IPs (10.x, 172.16-31.x, 192.168.x) are internal.
fixSteps: Sanitize server responses to remove internal IPs.

title: Stack Trace in Response
description: Stack trace exposed in page output.
riskImpact: Reveals code paths, file locations, and framework details.
explanation: Stack traces help attackers understand app internals.
fixSteps: Use custom error pages, log errors server-side only.

title: User Enumeration via Email
description: Error message reveals email existence.
riskImpact: Attackers can verify which emails are registered.
explanation: Different messages for valid/invalid emails enable enumeration.
fixSteps: Use generic messages like 'If this email exists...'

title: Verbose Error Messages
description: Detailed error messages in page output.
riskImpact: Technical details help attackers understand the system.
explanation: Error details reveal implementation specifics.
fixSteps: Show generic errors to users, log details server-side.

title: CDN Scripts Without Fallback
description: CDN-hosted scripts without local fallback.
riskImpact: Site breaks if CDN is unavailable or compromised.
explanation: SRI helps but local fallback ensures availability.
fixSteps: Add onerror fallback to local copy for critical scripts.

title: Potentially Outdated jQuery
description: jQuery version may have known vulnerabilities.
riskImpact: Old jQuery versions have XSS vulnerabilities.
explanation: jQuery < 3.5.0 has known security issues.
fixSteps: Update to latest jQuery version.

title: AngularJS 1.x Detected
description: End-of-life AngularJS framework detected.
riskImpact: No longer receives security updates.
explanation: AngularJS reached EOL Dec 2021, migrate to Angular.
fixSteps: Migrate to Angular (2+) or another modern framework.

title: Prototype.js Detected
description: Outdated Prototype.js library detected.
riskImpact: Unmaintained library with known vulnerabilities.
explanation: Prototype.js is no longer maintained.
fixSteps: Remove Prototype.js, use modern alternatives.

title: MooTools Detected
description: Outdated MooTools library detected.
riskImpact: Unmaintained library with potential security issues.
explanation: MooTools has limited maintenance.
fixSteps: Migrate to a modern, maintained framework.

title: Excessive document.cookie Access
description: Multiple document.cookie accesses detected.
riskImpact: Cookies may contain sensitive data accessible to scripts.
explanation: HttpOnly cookies prevent JavaScript access.
fixSteps: Use HttpOnly flag for sensitive cookies.

title: Credit Card Number Pattern
description: Potential credit card number in page source.
riskImpact: PCI DSS violation, data breach.
explanation: Card numbers should never appear in HTML.
fixSteps: Never display full card numbers, mask or tokenize.

title: SSN Pattern Detected
description: Social Security Number pattern in content.
riskImpact: Severe privacy violation, identity theft risk.
explanation: SSNs should never appear in page content.
fixSteps: Mask SSNs, never display in full.

title: Multiple Phone Numbers Exposed
description: Many phone numbers found in page source.
riskImpact: Privacy concern, potential data leak.
explanation: Bulk phone numbers may indicate data exposure.
fixSteps: Review if phone numbers should be displayed.

title: Many Email Addresses Exposed
description: Large number of email addresses in page.
riskImpact: Privacy concern, spam/phishing target list.
explanation: Bulk emails may indicate data exposure.
fixSteps: Review if emails should be displayed publicly.

title: Bearer Token Exposed in Source
description: Bearer token found in page source.
riskImpact: Direct credential exposure.
explanation: Bearer tokens grant API access.
fixSteps: Never embed tokens in HTML, use secure storage.

title: API Key in URL Parameter
description: API key found in URL parameter.
riskImpact: Key leaked in referrer, history, logs.
explanation: API keys in URLs are exposed in many places.
fixSteps: Pass API keys in headers instead of URLs.

title: AWS Credentials Pattern Detected
description: AWS access key pattern found in source.
riskImpact: Full AWS account compromise possible.
explanation: AWS keys grant access to cloud resources.
fixSteps: Rotate exposed credentials immediately.

title: Private Key Detected in Source
description: Private key pattern found in page.
riskImpact: Complete compromise of encrypted data.
explanation: Private keys should never be in HTML.
fixSteps: Rotate keys, investigate how they were exposed.

title: Database Connection String Exposed
description: Database connection string found in source.
riskImpact: Direct database access for attackers.
explanation: Connection strings contain credentials.
fixSteps: Rotate credentials, fix the exposure.

title: Stripe Key Exposed
description: Stripe API key pattern found.
riskImpact: May allow payment manipulation.
explanation: Live Stripe keys can process real payments.
fixSteps: Rotate keys, use publishable keys only in frontend.

title: Twilio Credentials Pattern
description: Twilio SID/token pattern found.
riskImpact: Unauthorized SMS/calls, billing fraud.
explanation: Twilio credentials allow API access.
fixSteps: Rotate credentials immediately.

title: SendGrid API Key Exposed
description: SendGrid API key pattern found.
riskImpact: Email sending abuse, phishing.
explanation: SendGrid keys allow email sending.
fixSteps: Rotate API key immediately.

title: Slack Webhook URL Exposed
description: Slack webhook URL found in source.
riskImpact: Spam messages to Slack channel.
explanation: Anyone with webhook URL can post messages.
fixSteps: Rotate webhook URL, keep it server-side.

title: Discord Webhook URL Exposed
description: Discord webhook URL found in source.
riskImpact: Spam messages to Discord channel.
explanation: Anyone with webhook URL can post messages.
fixSteps: Regenerate webhook, keep it server-side.

title: GitHub Token Pattern Detected
description: GitHub personal access token found.
riskImpact: Repository access, code manipulation.
explanation: GitHub tokens grant repo access.
fixSteps: Revoke token immediately, use fine-grained tokens.

title: Google API Key Exposed
description: Google API key found in source.
riskImpact: Quota theft, billing abuse.
explanation: Unrestricted Google keys can be abused.
fixSteps: Restrict key to specific APIs and referrers.

title: Mailchimp API Key Exposed
description: Mailchimp API key pattern found.
riskImpact: Email list access, campaign manipulation.
explanation: Mailchimp keys grant full account access.
fixSteps: Regenerate API key immediately.

title: Heroku API Key Exposed
description: Heroku API key pattern found.
riskImpact: Full Heroku account control.
explanation: Heroku keys allow app deployment and deletion.
fixSteps: Regenerate API key immediately.

title: NPM Token Exposed
description: NPM authentication token found.
riskImpact: Package publishing, supply chain attack.
explanation: NPM tokens allow package publishing.
fixSteps: Revoke token, publish with 2FA.

title: Docker Hub Token Exposed
description: Docker Hub credentials found.
riskImpact: Container image manipulation.
explanation: Docker tokens allow image push/pull.
fixSteps: Regenerate token immediately.

title: SQL Error Message Exposed
description: SQL error message found in response.
riskImpact: Reveals database structure, aids SQL injection.
explanation: SQL errors expose schema and query details.
fixSteps: Use custom error pages, log errors server-side.

title: NoSQL Error Message Exposed
description: NoSQL/MongoDB error found in response.
riskImpact: Reveals database type and query structure.
explanation: NoSQL errors aid injection attacks.
fixSteps: Use custom error pages, handle errors gracefully.

title: LDAP Error Message Exposed
description: LDAP error message in response.
riskImpact: Reveals directory structure.
explanation: LDAP errors may expose user enumeration.
fixSteps: Return generic error messages.

title: XML Parser Error Exposed
description: XML parser error in response.
riskImpact: May reveal XXE vulnerability.
explanation: XML errors can indicate parser misconfiguration.
fixSteps: Handle XML errors gracefully.

title: JSON Array at Top Level
description: API returns JSON array as top-level response.
riskImpact: Legacy browsers vulnerable to JSON hijacking.
explanation: Older browsers could include JSON in script tags.
fixSteps: Wrap array in object, or use CORS properly.

title: JSONP Endpoint Detected
description: JSONP callback parameter found.
riskImpact: JSONP can leak data cross-origin.
explanation: JSONP bypasses same-origin policy by design.
fixSteps: Replace JSONP with CORS for cross-origin requests.

title: Potential DOM Clobbering
description: HTML elements with IDs that could clobber globals.
riskImpact: JavaScript globals can be overwritten.
explanation: HTML id/name attributes create global variables.
fixSteps: Avoid relying on global variable names from DOM.

title: Iframe with srcdoc Attribute
description: Iframe using srcdoc for inline HTML.
riskImpact: Inline HTML in iframes can be complex to secure.
explanation: srcdoc allows HTML directly in iframe.
fixSteps: Ensure srcdoc content is properly sanitized.

title: Iframe Sandbox Allows Scripts
description: Sandboxed iframe with allow-scripts.
riskImpact: Sandboxed content can still execute JavaScript.
explanation: allow-scripts re-enables JavaScript in sandbox.
fixSteps: Only add allow-scripts if absolutely necessary.

title: SVG with Script Content
description: SVG containing script elements.
riskImpact: XSS via SVG images.
explanation: SVG can contain executable JavaScript.
fixSteps: Sanitize SVG uploads, use CSP.

title: Script with data: URI
description: Script tag using data: URI source.
riskImpact: Inline script execution via data URI.
explanation: data: URIs can embed script content.
fixSteps: Block data: in CSP script-src.

title: Script from blob: URL
description: Script loaded from blob: URL.
riskImpact: Dynamic script generation.
explanation: Blob URLs can be used to execute dynamic scripts.
fixSteps: Consider blocking blob: in CSP.

title: Links Missing noopener
description: External links without rel=noopener.
riskImpact: Linked page can access window.opener.
explanation: target=_blank without noopener leaks opener.
fixSteps: Add rel='noopener noreferrer' to external links.

title: Password Field Missing autocomplete
description: Password field without autocomplete attribute.
riskImpact: Browser may not offer to save password.
explanation: autocomplete helps password managers.
fixSteps: Set autocomplete='current-password' or 'new-password'.

title: Form Disables Autocomplete
description: Form has autocomplete=off.
riskImpact: Prevents password manager functionality.
explanation: Users may enter weaker manual passwords.
fixSteps: Allow autocomplete unless there's a specific reason.

title: Password Field with Short maxlength
description: Password input with very short maxlength.
riskImpact: Artificially limits password strength.
explanation: Short passwords are easier to crack.
fixSteps: Allow at least 64-128 character passwords.

title: Hidden Password Input Field
description: Password field with type=hidden.
riskImpact: May expose password in page source.
explanation: Hidden inputs are visible in HTML source.
fixSteps: Never store passwords in hidden fields.

title: Password Field Type is Text
description: Password stored in text input instead of password type.
riskImpact: Password visible on screen.
explanation: Password fields should mask input.
fixSteps: Use type='password' for password inputs.

title: Sensitive Data in Readonly Field
description: Sensitive data in readonly input field.
riskImpact: Users can still copy, data in DOM.
explanation: Readonly doesn't prevent data access.
fixSteps: Consider masking or removing sensitive readonly data.

title: File Upload Without Type Restrictions
description: File input without accept attribute.
riskImpact: Malicious file uploads possible.
explanation: Accept attribute provides client-side hint.
fixSteps: Set accept attribute and validate on server.

title: Multiple File Upload Enabled
description: File input allows multiple files.
riskImpact: DoS via many file uploads.
explanation: Multiple files increases attack surface.
fixSteps: Limit file count and total size server-side.

title: Source Map Files Exposed
description: JavaScript source maps are publicly accessible.
riskImpact: Reveals original source code.
explanation: Source maps help reverse-engineer code.
fixSteps: Remove source maps in production or restrict access.

title: Developer Comments in HTML
description: HTML comments containing developer notes.
riskImpact: May reveal internal information.
explanation: Comments are visible in page source.
fixSteps: Remove developer comments in production.

title: TODO/FIXME Comments Exposed
description: TODO or FIXME comments in page source.
riskImpact: Reveals incomplete security work.
explanation: May indicate known issues.
fixSteps: Address and remove TODO comments before production.

title: Iframe Missing Lazy Loading
description: Below-fold iframes without loading=lazy.
riskImpact: Performance impact.
explanation: Lazy loading defers iframe loading.
fixSteps: Add loading='lazy' to non-critical iframes.

title: Missing Preconnect for Third-Party
description: Third-party resources without preconnect hint.
riskImpact: Slower third-party resource loading.
explanation: Preconnect establishes early connections.
fixSteps: Add preconnect for critical third-party origins.

title: Missing DNS Prefetch for Third-Party
description: External domains without dns-prefetch.
riskImpact: Slower DNS resolution.
explanation: DNS prefetch resolves domains early.
fixSteps: Add dns-prefetch for third-party domains.

title: Critical Script Missing SRI
description: CDN script without Subresource Integrity.
riskImpact: CDN compromise affects your site.
explanation: SRI verifies resource integrity.
fixSteps: Add integrity attribute to CDN scripts.

title: CDN Stylesheet Missing SRI
description: CDN CSS without Subresource Integrity.
riskImpact: CSS injection via compromised CDN.
explanation: SRI applies to stylesheets too.
fixSteps: Add integrity attribute to CDN stylesheets.

title: Password input without autocomplete='current-password'
description: Password inputs should declare autocomplete='current-password' so password managers can fill them.
evidence: Async / body / header check for password-input-toggle.
riskImpact: Add autocomplete='current-password' to password fields
explanation: Password inputs should declare autocomplete='current-password' so password managers can fill them.
fixSteps: Add autocomplete='current-password' to password fields

title: Email input without autocomplete='email'
description: Email inputs should declare autocomplete='email' so password managers / iOS can offer to fill them.
evidence: Async / body / header check for email-input-no-autocomplete.
riskImpact: Add autocomplete='email' to email fields
explanation: Email inputs should declare autocomplete='email' so password managers / iOS can offer to fill them.
fixSteps: Add autocomplete='email' to email fields

title: Credit-card input without autocomplete='cc-number'
description: Credit-card number inputs should declare autocomplete='cc-number'.
evidence: Async / body / header check for cc-input-no-autocomplete.
riskImpact: Add autocomplete='cc-number' to credit-card number fields
explanation: Credit-card number inputs should declare autocomplete='cc-number'.
fixSteps: Add autocomplete='cc-number' to credit-card number fields

title: Search input without type='search'
description: Search inputs should be type='search' so the browser can offer semantic features.
evidence: Async / body / header check for search-input-no-type.
riskImpact: Add type='search' to search inputs
explanation: Search inputs should be type='search' so the browser can offer semantic features.
fixSteps: Add type='search' to search inputs

title: Tel input without autocomplete='tel'
description: Tel inputs should declare autocomplete='tel'.
evidence: Async / body / header check for tel-input-no-autocomplete.
riskImpact: Add autocomplete='tel'
explanation: Tel inputs should declare autocomplete='tel'.
fixSteps: Add autocomplete='tel'

title: Image missing alt attribute
description: Every should have an alt attribute (even if empty for decorative images).
evidence: Async / body / header check for img-no-alt.
riskImpact: Add alt='' for decorative or alt='description' for meaningful images
explanation: Every should have an alt attribute (even if empty for decorative images).
fixSteps: Add alt='' for decorative or alt='description' for meaningful images

title: <a target=_blank> without rel=noopener
description: External links opened in a new tab without rel=noopener allow the new tab to navigate the original via window.opener.
evidence: Async / body / header check for link-no-rel.
riskImpact: Add rel='noopener noreferrer' to all target=_blank links
explanation: External links opened in a new tab without rel=noopener allow the new tab to navigate the original via window.opener.
fixSteps: Add rel='noopener noreferrer' to all target=_blank links

title: Form submits to insecure URL
description: <form action='http://...'> on an HTTPS page downgrades credentials on submit.
evidence: Async / body / header check for form-no-action-https.
riskImpact: Use relative paths or absolute https:// URLs in form actions
explanation: <form action='http://...'> on an HTTPS page downgrades credentials on submit.
fixSteps: Use relative paths or absolute https:// URLs in form actions

title: Meta refresh without URL
description: <meta http-equiv=refresh content='5'> without url= is informational; with url= it's a redirect.
evidence: Async / body / header check for meta-redirect-no-url.
riskImpact: Use HTTP 301/302 instead of meta refresh
explanation: <meta http-equiv=refresh content='5'> without url= is informational; with url= it's a redirect.
fixSteps: Use HTTP 301/302 instead of meta refresh

title: Iframe missing allowfullscreen attribute
description: Iframes without allowfullscreen can't fill the viewport on mobile.
evidence: Async / body / header check for iframe-missing-allowfullscreen.
riskImpact: Add allowfullscreen attribute
explanation: Iframes without allowfullscreen can't fill the viewport on mobile.
fixSteps: Add allowfullscreen attribute

title: Iframe missing loading='lazy'
description: Off-screen iframes should load lazily.
evidence: Async / body / header check for iframe-missing-loading-lazy.
riskImpact: Add loading='lazy' to below-the-fold iframes
explanation: Off-screen iframes should load lazily.
fixSteps: Add loading='lazy' to below-the-fold iframes

title: Login form missing autocomplete='username'
description: Username inputs should declare autocomplete='username' so password managers can fill them.
evidence: Async / body / header check for autocomplete-username.
riskImpact: Add autocomplete='username'
explanation: Username inputs should declare autocomplete='username' so password managers can fill them.
fixSteps: Add autocomplete='username'

title: Image URL is protocol-relative
description: //cdn.example.com/x.png is fine but consider explicit https:// for clarity.
evidence: Async / body / header check for image-protocol-relative.
riskImpact: Prefer explicit https:// URLs in modern apps
explanation: //cdn.example.com/x.png is fine but consider explicit https:// for clarity.
fixSteps: Prefer explicit https:// URLs in modern apps

title: Open Graph image URL not HTTPS
description: og:image should use HTTPS to avoid mixed content on social embeds.
evidence: Async / body / header check for open-graph-image-not-https.
riskImpact: Use https:// for og:image URLs
explanation: og:image should use HTTPS to avoid mixed content on social embeds.
fixSteps: Use https:// for og:image URLs

title: <link rel='canonical'> Missing
description: Missing canonical link makes SEO harder and lets scrapers re-host without attribution.
evidence: Async / body / header check for canonical-link-missing.
riskImpact: Add <link rel='canonical' href='...'> to <head>
explanation: Missing canonical link makes SEO harder and lets scrapers re-host without attribution.
fixSteps: Add <link rel='canonical' href='...'> to <head>

title: <meta name='viewport'> Missing
description: Pages without a viewport meta render poorly on mobile.
evidence: Async / body / header check for viewport-meta-missing.
riskImpact: Add <meta name='viewport' content='width=device-width, initial-scale=1'>
explanation: Pages without a viewport meta render poorly on mobile.
fixSteps: Add <meta name='viewport' content='width=device-width, initial-scale=1'>

title: <meta charset> Missing
description: Pages without an explicit charset risk XSS via UTF-7 exploits (legacy IE).
evidence: Async / body / header check for charset-meta-missing.
riskImpact: Add <meta charset='utf-8'> as the first element of <head>
explanation: Pages without an explicit charset risk XSS via UTF-7 exploits (legacy IE).
fixSteps: Add <meta charset='utf-8'> as the first element of <head>

title: <!DOCTYPE html> Missing
description: Pages without DOCTYPE html trigger quirks mode in legacy browsers.
evidence: Async / body / header check for doctype-missing.
riskImpact: Add <!DOCTYPE html> at the top of the document
explanation: Pages without DOCTYPE html trigger quirks mode in legacy browsers.
fixSteps: Add <!DOCTYPE html> at the top of the document

title: Inline style attribute
description: Inline styles can't be inspected by CSP and complicate theming.
evidence: Async / body / header check for inline-style-attr.
riskImpact: Move inline styles to external stylesheets
explanation: Inline styles can't be inspected by CSP and complicate theming.
fixSteps: Move inline styles to external stylesheets

title: target=_blank without rel=noopener
description: Without rel=noopener the new tab can navigate the source page via window.opener.
evidence: Async / body / header check for target-blank-no-noopener.
riskImpact: Add rel='noopener noreferrer'
explanation: Without rel=noopener the new tab can navigate the source page via window.opener.
fixSteps: Add rel='noopener noreferrer'

title: Plain mailto: link
description: Plain mailto: links get harvested by spam bots.
evidence: Async / body / header check for email-mailto-spam.
riskImpact: Use a contact form instead of a plain mailto: link
explanation: Plain mailto: links get harvested by spam bots.
fixSteps: Use a contact form instead of a plain mailto: link

title: Third-party iframe without sandbox
description: Third-party widgets (Stripe, YouTube) loaded without sandbox can run arbitrary scripts.
evidence: Async / body / header check for iframe-third-party-without-sandbox.
riskImpact: Add sandbox='allow-scripts allow-same-origin allow-forms' to third-party iframes
explanation: Third-party widgets (Stripe, YouTube) loaded without sandbox can run arbitrary scripts.
fixSteps: Add sandbox='allow-scripts allow-same-origin allow-forms' to third-party iframes

title: Autofocus combined with positive tabindex
description: Combining autofocus with tabindex > 0 produces confusing focus order for keyboard and screen reader users.
evidence: Async / body / header check for autofocus-positive-tabindex.
riskImpact: Remove positive tabindex from autofocused elements; let the natural tab order stand
explanation: Combining autofocus with tabindex > 0 produces confusing focus order for keyboard and screen reader users.
fixSteps: Remove positive tabindex from autofocused elements; let the natural tab order stand

title: aria-hidden=true with focusable children
description: When aria-hidden=true is applied to a container that still contains focusable elements, keyboard users can tab into invisible controls.
evidence: Async / body / header check for aria-hidden-focusable-children.
riskImpact: Add tabindex=-1 to focusable descendants, or remove aria-hidden from the parent
explanation: When aria-hidden=true is applied to a container that still contains focusable elements, keyboard users can tab into invisible controls.
fixSteps: Add tabindex=-1 to focusable descendants, or remove aria-hidden from the parent

title: formnovalidate attribute on submit button
description: A submit button with formnovalidate bypasses HTML5 input constraints (required, pattern, type=email). Server-side validation must catch this.
evidence: Async / body / header check for form-formnovalidate-bypass.
riskImpact: Always validate on the server; never rely on browser-side form validation alone
explanation: A submit button with formnovalidate bypasses HTML5 input constraints (required, pattern, type=email). Server-side validation must catch this.
fixSteps: Always validate on the server; never rely on browser-side form validation alone

title: Form action uses javascript: scheme
description: A <form action="javascript:..."> executes JavaScript on submit and is a classic XSS vector if the action is partially attacker-controlled.
evidence: Async / body / header check for form-action-javascript-scheme.
riskImpact: Replace action="javascript:..." with action="/endpoint" and handle submit via JS or server
explanation: A <form action="javascript:..."> executes JavaScript on submit and is a classic XSS vector if the action is partially attacker-controlled.
fixSteps: Replace action="javascript:..." with action="/endpoint" and handle submit via JS or server

title: Form action uses mailto: scheme
description: A <form action="mailto:..."> opens the user's mail client, leaks the recipient address, and is unreliable for data submission.
evidence: Async / body / header check for form-action-mailto-scheme.
riskImpact: Use a server endpoint or contact form instead of mailto: for form submission
explanation: A <form action="mailto:..."> opens the user's mail client, leaks the recipient address, and is unreliable for data submission.
fixSteps: Use a server endpoint or contact form instead of mailto: for form submission

title: Form action uses tel: scheme
description: A <form action="tel:..."> triggers the user's dialer. Acceptable for click-to-call buttons but not for full form submission.
evidence: Async / body / header check for form-action-tel-scheme.
riskImpact: Use tel: on anchor tags or buttons, not on form action attributes
explanation: A <form action="tel:..."> triggers the user's dialer. Acceptable for click-to-call buttons but not for full form submission.
fixSteps: Use tel: on anchor tags or buttons, not on form action attributes

title: Iframe srcdoc without sandbox attribute
description: Iframes using srcdoc without the sandbox attribute run inline HTML in the parent origin's context, expanding the attack surface.
evidence: Async / body / header check for iframe-srcdoc-no-sandbox.
riskImpact: Add sandbox='allow-scripts allow-same-origin' (or stricter) to all srcdoc iframes
explanation: Iframes using srcdoc without the sandbox attribute run inline HTML in the parent origin's context, expanding the attack surface.
fixSteps: Add sandbox='allow-scripts allow-same-origin' (or stricter) to all srcdoc iframes

title: Sandboxed iframe allows both scripts and same-origin
description: Combining sandbox='allow-scripts allow-same-origin' removes most of the sandbox protection and lets the iframe access the parent origin.
evidence: Async / body / header check for iframe-allow-scripts-allow-same-origin.
riskImpact: Drop allow-same-origin (or allow-scripts) - together they fully re-enable same-origin script execution
explanation: Combining sandbox='allow-scripts allow-same-origin' removes most of the sandbox protection and lets the iframe access the parent origin.
fixSteps: Drop allow-same-origin (or allow-scripts) - together they fully re-enable same-origin script execution

title: SVG with inline onload handler
description: An <svg onload="..."> attribute fires JavaScript when the SVG renders and is equivalent to inline script for XSS purposes.
evidence: Async / body / header check for svg-onload-handler.
riskImpact: Move event handlers to addEventListener in external JS; sanitize SVG uploads
explanation: An <svg onload="..."> attribute fires JavaScript when the SVG renders and is equivalent to inline script for XSS purposes.
fixSteps: Move event handlers to addEventListener in external JS; sanitize SVG uploads

title: SVG with external entity reference (XXE)
description: An <svg> with an external entity reference like <!ENTITY xxe SYSTEM "file:///..."> can leak local files when the SVG is rendered server-side.
evidence: Async / body / header check for svg-external-entity-reference.
riskImpact: Strip DOCTYPE and external entities when ingesting SVG; use a hardened parser
explanation: An <svg> with an external entity reference like <!ENTITY xxe SYSTEM "file:///..."> can leak local files when the SVG is rendered server-side.
fixSteps: Strip DOCTYPE and external entities when ingesting SVG; use a hardened parser
